content:
Formatting
* Please study the formatting syntax on this page: https://www.codingame.com/playgrounds/40701/help-center/statement , and format your statement properly as described in the points below.

Goal
* Please format 1.0, 0.5 and 0.3 as {{constants}}.

Input
* (optional) Consider simplifying "First line" and "The following N lines" to "Line 1" and "Next N lines".

* Please format the line numbers in <<bold>> (e.g. <<First line>> or <<Line 1>>).

* Please format all instances of "N" as a [[variable]].

* Please add "[[age]]" after "one integer".

Output
* Since the problem only involves summing only numbers that have one decimal place, there's no need for any rounding. The result will naturally have one decimal place as well.

* Because of that, please replace "rounded to" with "shown with" to better reflect what's actually required.

* Please also remove "(e.g., 2.3)", as the Example section already illustrates the format required.

Constraints
* Please format "N" and "age" as [[variables]].

Cases
* Please remove the trailing newlines from the input of the cases below:
  • Test 1
  • Test 2
  • Validator 4

* Test 3: Please add three more ages: 13, 14 and 14.

* Validator 3: Please add two more ages: 13 and 15.

* Test 4: Please add two more ages at the end: 0 and 14.

* Validator 4: Please add two more ages at the end: 0 and 14.

Thanks.

"Since the problem only involves summing only numbers that have one decimal place, there's no need for any rounding. The result will naturally have one decimal place as well."

Not really; in Python, 0.3 + 0.3 + 0.3 = 0.8999999999999999. Rounding is still required to guard against floating point representation issues.
Indeed. Thanks for pointing that out.
